title: Blowfish

{{Quote|Nikaede, this was a great idea!|Drake Paulsen shouting in joy as he gazed upon a banquet of food, including deep-fried Issori blowfish snouts|<ref name="Idol" />}}
'''Blowfish''' were a relatively common [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Fish/Legends|fish]] notable for their puffiness. They could be found on multiple [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including lush worlds like [[Issor]] and [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], or even more arid planet's like [[Lok]] and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Through the act of [[Fishing/Legends|fishing]], it was possible to lure and catch blowfish and use their meat as ingredients in various [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]]. During the [[Moon Festival]] of [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] on [[Omman Minor]], the [[Socorran]] [[Drake Paulsen]] and [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion [[Nikaede Celso]] were served a generous portion of deep-fired [[Issori]] blowfish [[Nose/Legends|snouts]] in [[Feyodor's Tavern]].

==Biology and appearance==
Although some [[Species/Legends|species]] of blowfish were considered rare, they were for the most part quite common. They could be found swimming in the bodies of [[Water/Legends|water]] of multiple [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]], [[Endor/Legends|Endor]],<ref name="AED" /> [[Issor]],<ref name="Idol" /> [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], [[Rori/Legends|Rori]], [[Talus/Legends|Talus]], and [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. They could even be found in the [[Sulfur/Legends|sulfur]] pools and rivers of [[Lok]], and in the few oases of the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. Relatively small, with [[Color/Legends|green]] skin of varying hues and dark beady [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], one of the blowfish's most distinguishing features were their puffiness. By affixing various forms of bait to a [[fishing pole]], including [[grub]]s, [[Insect/Legends|insects]] or [[Worm/Legends|worms]], it was possible for individuals to lure and reel blowfish out of the water through the act of [[Fishing/Legends|fishing]]. After being caught, blowfish meat could be used as ingredients in various [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]].<ref name="AED" /> Their [[Nose/Legends|snouts]] could also be deep-fried and served as a meal of their own.<ref name="Idol" />

==History==
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars Galaxies}}
During the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] scoured the galaxy for fish, including a particularly rare variety of blowfish. Upon catching the blowfish, and six other species of rare fish, the spacer became recognized as a novice angler.<ref name="AED" />
{{Endgame}}
During the [[Moon Festival]]<ref name="Idol" /> of [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> on [[Omman Minor]], the [[Socorran]] [[Drake Paulsen]] and [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion [[Nikaede Celso]] patronized the upper level of [[Feyodor's Tavern]]. While there, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Chef/Legends|chef]] [[Feyodor]] served the duo an exquisite feast, including a generous portion of deep-fried [[Issori]] blowfish [[Nose/Legends|snouts]], alongside a stuffed [[Uln bird]] glazed with a light mist of [[Zsajhira berry juice]], and several other accompaniments.<ref name="Idol" />

==Behind the scenes==
Blowfish first appeared as a deep-fried meal in ''[[Idol Intentions]]'', a short story written by [[Patricia A. Jackson]] that was originally published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 12|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 12]] by [[West End Games]] in [[February]] [[1997]].<ref name="Idol" /> They were later included as [[W:c:swg:Fishing|fishable]] [[W:c:swg:Creatures & enemies|creatures]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]], prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="AED" /><ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Players who used [[W:c:swg:Fishing pole|fishing poles]] could catch blowfish on all the original [[W:c:swg:Planet|worlds]] of [[W:c:swg:Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies'']], including [[W:c:swg:Corellia|Corellia]], [[W:c:swg:Dantooine|Dantooine]], [[W:c:swg:Dathomir|Dathomir]], [[W:c:swg:Endor|Endor]], [[W:c:swg:Lok|Lok]], [[W:c:swg:Naboo|Naboo]], [[W:c:swg:Rori|Rori]], [[W:c:swg:Talus|Talus]], [[W:c:swg:Tatooine|Tatooine]], and [[W:c:swg:Yavin IV|Yavin 4]].<ref name="AED" /> According to the game files, blowfish could have also been found on [[Taanab/Legends|Taanab]], but that world was cut from the final release of ''Galaxies'', rendering that particular information ambiguous in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Canon|continuity]].<ref name="TRE">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|directory tree files}}</ref>

As with most other fish in the game, blowfish could be [[W:c:swg:Harvesting|harvested]] for their [[W:c:swg:Creature resources|resources]].<ref name="AED" /> With the release of [[W:c:swg:Chapter 7 Notes for Nov 1 2007|Chapter 7: ''A Collection of Heroes'']] on [[November 1]], [[2007]], a [[W:c:swg:Collection|collection]] [[W:c:swg:Quest|quest]] titled [[W:c:swg:The Reel Life|''The Reel Life'']] was added to the game.<ref name="AED" /><ref name="Chapter7">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67485&month=102007|text=Chapter 7: A Collection of Heroes}}</ref> To complete this collection, players had to successfully catch seven fish, including a "Rare Blowfish" which could be found on any of the original aforementioned worlds. Completing this collection would award the player with the [[W:c:swg:Title|title]] of "Novice Angler." Although players could choose not to complete this collection, this article assumes 100% game completion.<ref name="AED" />
